title: [YouTube] Fix 403 error and throttling
body: Decrypting `signatureCipher` to fix the recent 403 error, as well as to solve the long-standing download speed throttling issue (#2950). This was made possible thanks to the lightweight JavaScript interpreter [dukpy](https://pypi.org/project/dukpy/).

step_plan.summary: Update the YouTube extractor to correctly decrypt signatureCipher/cipher-protected stream URLs using the player JS executed via dukpy (with a safe fallback), robustly parse cipher params with parse_qs, and append the computed signature using the stream's 'sp' parameter to fix 403 errors and throttling.

steps.operation: Replace fragile JS->Python exec-based signature decoder with dukpy-backed JS evaluation (with fallback)
steps.reason: Current implementation translates player JS into Python via regex and exec(), which is fragile and fails for newer player JS shapes, causing 403s and throttling. Using dukpy.evaljs to run the real player JS (with a small wrapper that calls the decipher function) provides robust, future-proof signature computation. Wrap dukpy import in try/except ImportError to preserve backward compatibility and fallback to the existing translator+exec path when dukpy is unavailable.
steps.side_effects: Changes the runtime dependency on dukpy (see packaging note below). Tests that assert the internals of the old translation may break and should be updated. Downstream code that expects the old s_to_sig interface will not change, but timing/behavior of signature computation may differ slightly (should be functionally identical). Also requires adding code to handle JS wrapper creation and safe evaluation; ensure exceptions from dukpy are caught and fall back correctly. Update setup.py or project metadata to add dukpy as an optional dependency or install_requires entry so environments can opt-in; however, runtime will still function without dukpy because of the fallback, so packaging changes are optional but recommended.

steps.operation: Make parsing of ciphered stream params robust and ensure player JS is fetched before decryption
steps.reason: The extractor currently parses signatureCipher via ad-hoc string splitting which mis-decodes URL-encoded components and fails for the new 'cipher' key. Using urllib.parse.parse_qs (or urllib.parse.parse_qsl then dict conversion) properly decodes 's', 'sp', 'url', and related params. Also some code paths call s_to_sig before the html5player JS (self.js) is guaranteed to be fetched — ensure prepare calls the method that fetches/sets self.js (existing get_content/html5player-fetch helpers) prior to attempting to decipher signatures. When assembling stream URLs, use stream.get('sp', 'sig') to determine the parameter name for the signature instead of hard-coding '&sig=' .
steps.side_effects: Correct parsing affects all streams that use signatureCipher/cipher; previously-broken streams should now work. Tests that relied on previous brittle parsing must be updated. Ensuring player JS fetching will add extra network fetch in some code paths (or reuse an already-cached self.js) — this may slightly increase prepare latency for some videos but prevents 403s. Downstream consumers of stream objects should see normalized fields (decoded 'url', 's', 'sp'), which may alter test fixtures. No API changes to public interfaces.

steps.operation: Use stream's sp parameter when appending decrypted signature and ensure player JS availability before use
steps.reason: Final URL assembly currently appends the computed signature with a hard-coded '&sig=' which is incorrect for streams that use a different parameter name (the 'sp' field). Use '&' + stream.get('sp', 'sig') + '=' + sig to append the signature. Also ensure that extract ensures the player JS is fetched if s_to_sig needs it (call the same helper used in prepare or trigger get_content if necessary) so the dukpy evaluation has the required JS available.
steps.side_effects: Fixes incorrect signature parameter name and prevents generating malformed URLs that lead to 403s or throttling; fixes are limited to URL assembly. Tests that check exact generated URLs must be updated to expect the sp-aware parameter. Ensuring JS fetch mirrors behavior added in prepare — may result in additional network fetchs in some paths if prepare was not previously called. No change to public extractor API.
